skill.name: lark-minutes
skill.description: Get Feishu/Lark Minutes metadata including title, cover, duration, and AI-generated outputs like summaries, action items, and chapters
skill.summary: Retrieve meeting minutes information from Feishu/Lark platform using official CLI tools
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: This skill is documentation-only (SKILL.md markdown file) describing how to use the official Lark CLI tool. Static analyzer detected 30 patterns but all are FALSE POSITIVES - the patterns appear in documentation examples, not executable code. No actual command execution, network calls, or file operations exist in this skill. The skill references the official lark-cli tool which requires separate installation and authentication.

low_findings.title: External Command Documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md documents usage of lark-cli external command tool. This is documentation only, not executable code. The actual CLI tool must be installed separately and requires user authentication.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly declares dependency on lark-cli binary and provides CLI help reference. This is legitimate documentation for an official tool.

content.value_statement: Quickly retrieve meeting minutes information from Feishu/Lark platform. This skill helps you access title, cover image, duration, and AI-generated summaries without manual navigation.
actual_capabilities: Extract minute_token from Feishu/Lark minutes URLs
Retrieve meeting minutes metadata including title, cover, and duration
Query owner information and minutes URL
Display links to related AI-generated outputs like summaries and action items
limitations: Requires lark-cli tool to be installed and configured separately
Requires valid Feishu/Lark authentication and API permissions
Only provides metadata, not full transcript content (use lark-cli vc +notes for content)
Works only with Feishu/Lark platform minutes, not other meeting tools

use_cases.title: Project Manager Tracking Meeting Records
use_cases.description: Quickly retrieve metadata for multiple meeting minutes to organize and categorize project discussions. Get titles, durations, and links to summaries for documentation purposes.

use_cases.title: Assistant Compiling Meeting Archives
use_cases.description: Help executives access their meeting history by fetching minutes information. Extract key details like duration and ownership for reporting and time tracking.

use_cases.title: Developer Building Meeting Integrations
use_cases.description: Integrate Feishu minutes data into custom dashboards or workflows. Use the CLI commands to programmatically access meeting metadata for automation scripts.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Minutes Info Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the basic information for this Feishu minutes link: https://company.feishu.cn/minutes/abc123xyz. I need the title, duration, and owner.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick metadata retrieval for a single meeting

prompt_templates.title: Extract Token from URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the minute_token from this URL: https://domain.feishu.cn/minutes/obc123456?project=xxx. Then use it to query the minutes information.

prompt_templates.title: List Related Outputs
prompt_templates.prompt: For minutes token obcnhijv43vq6bcsl5xasfb2, show me what AI-generated outputs are available like summaries, action items, and chapters. Just show the links, do not read the content yet.
prompt_templates.scenario: Discovering available outputs without fetching content

prompt_templates.title: Batch Minutes Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: I have 5 meeting minutes URLs from this week. For each one, extract the token and get the title and duration. Present them in a table format for my weekly report.

output_examples.input: Get info for minutes token obcnq3b9jl72l83w4f14xxxx
What outputs are available for this meeting?
output: - Summary: Link to AI-generated meeting summary
- Action Items: Link to extracted tasks and owners
- Chapters: Link to auto-generated meeting sections
- Transcript: Link to full verbatim transcript

best_practices: Always verify the minute_token is extracted correctly from the URL, removing any query parameters
Run lark-cli schema minutes.minutes.get first to understand the response structure before making requests
Use the minutes.get method for metadata only; use lark-cli vc +notes for accessing actual content like transcripts and summaries
anti_patterns: Do not guess parameter formats - always check schema first with lark-cli schema
Do not attempt to access minutes content without proper authentication setup
Do not use this skill for accessing meeting content - it only provides metadata, not transcripts or summaries

faq.question: What is a minute_token and how do I get it?
faq.answer: A minute_token is the unique identifier for a Feishu/Lark meeting minutes. You can extract it from the minutes URL - it is the last segment after /minutes/. For example, from https://company.feishu.cn/minutes/abc123xyz, the token is abc123xyz.

faq.question: Can this skill retrieve meeting transcripts?
faq.answer: No, this skill only retrieves metadata (title, cover, duration, owner). For transcripts, summaries, action items, and chapters, use lark-cli vc +notes instead.

faq.question: What permissions do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need the minutes:minutes:readonly scope permission in your Feishu/Lark API configuration. The lark-cli tool must also be installed and authenticated.

faq.question: Can I use this with URLs that have query parameters?
faq.answer: Yes, extract only the path portion of the URL. For example, from https://domain.feishu.cn/minutes/obc123456?project=xxx, extract obc123456 as the token and ignore the query parameters.

faq.question: How do I check what parameters the API accepts?
faq.answer: Run lark-cli schema minutes.minutes.get to see the exact parameter structure and response format before making API calls.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with both Feishu and Lark?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill works with both Feishu (China) and Lark (international) platforms as they share the same underlying API structure through the lark-cli tool.
